title: Argentinian Kabanos Crumble
description: A twist on traditional breakfast dishes, featuring Polish kabanos sausage and double-rise bread, perfect for a Jain-friendly brunch option.

ingredients.name: double-rise bread
ingredients.notes: cubed, day-old bread for best results

instructions: Preheat oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit (190 degrees Celsius).
In a large skillet, heat 2 tablespoons of butter over medium heat. Add chopped onion and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add minced garlic and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, until fragrant.
In a separate mixing bowl, combine cubed bread, kabanos sausage, melted butter, and grated cheddar cheese (if using).
Transfer bread mixture to a 9x13 inch baking dish and bake for 25-30 minutes, or until golden brown and crispy.
Garnish with chopped parsley and serve immediately.
Adjust oven rack to middle position and increase temperature to broil (high).
Broil for an additional 2-3 minutes, or until top is golden brown and crispy, watching closely to avoid burning.
Let cool for 5 minutes before serving.
Serve warm, with additional cheddar cheese (if desired).
Reheat in oven at 350 degrees Fahrenheit (175 degrees Celsius) for 5-7 minutes, or until warmed through.
Adjust seasoning as needed, and serve hot.
Garnish with additional parsley, if desired.
Serve warm, with a side of fresh fruit or salad, for a satisfying breakfast or brunch option.
Garnish with additional parsley, if desired.
Serve warm, with a side of toast or hash browns, for a filling breakfast or brunch option.
